app.name: Ion
app.one_liner: A CLI toolbox for agentic developers to manage reusable AI agent skills as versioned, installable packages.

screens.name: Main CLI Interface
screens.purpose: Provide access to all skill and project management commands through a terminal interface.

rust_commands.name: ion_add
rust_commands.purpose: Add a skill from a source or install all skills defined in Ion.toml.

rust_commands.name: ion_cache_gc
rust_commands.purpose: Clean up stale cached repositories and artifacts.

rust_commands.name: ion_config
rust_commands.purpose: View or modify Ion configuration settings.

rust_commands.name: ion_project_init
rust_commands.purpose: Initialize a new Ion.toml configuration file with targets.

rust_commands.name: ion_project_migrate
rust_commands.purpose: Migrate legacy skill configurations to current format.

rust_commands.name: ion_remove
rust_commands.purpose: Remove a specified skill; supports preview mode with --json and execution with --yes.

rust_commands.name: ion_search
rust_commands.purpose: Search registries and GitHub for available skills.

rust_commands.name: ion_skill_eject
rust_commands.purpose: Convert a remote skill into an editable local copy.

rust_commands.name: ion_skill_info
rust_commands.purpose: Display detailed metadata about a specific skill.

rust_commands.name: ion_skill_link
rust_commands.purpose: Link a local directory as a skill for development.

rust_commands.name: ion_skill_new
rust_commands.purpose: Create a new local skill scaffold.

rust_commands.name: ion_skill_validate
rust_commands.purpose: Validate skill definitions against internal schema or rules.

rust_commands.name: ion_update
rust_commands.purpose: Update one or all skills to their latest versions.

mvp_plan: week 1: Build two-stage execution (preview + confirm) for destructive ops
week 1: Enable local skill linking and ejection
week 1: Implement core skill add/remove/update lifecycle
week 1: Support JSON output mode for all commands
acceptance_tests: All commands produce valid JSON when invoked with `--json` flag.
Running `ion --json remove my-skill --yes` removes the skill and exits with code 0.
Running `ion --json remove my-skill` returns a preview payload and exits with code 2.
User can initialize a project with `ion project init` and generate Ion.toml.
User can install a skill via `ion add <source>` and see it listed in `ion skill list`.
